meaning: n.<美>分阶段(或逐步)的停止(或结束、完成等)
examples: However, the industry may have to pay a higher price for tobacco once the phaseout begins.
The U. S. phaseout of lead in gasoline was completed in 1996; similar bans are being implemented worldwide.
If the CFC phaseout proceeds on schedule, the atmosphere should start repairing itself by the year 2000, say scientists.
Stay tuned for posts on the EITC and phaseout ranges, which can also penalize couples.
They often avoid the 3.8% tax, the Pease limit and the Personal Exemption Phaseout as well.
